source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A block slides down a surface to the horizontal as shown in figure. The coefficient of kinetic friction is \(0.25\). A string attached to the block is wrapped around a flywheel on a fixed axis at \(O\). The flywheel has mass \(25.0\text{ kg}\) and moment of inertia \(0.500\text{ kg}\cdot\text{m}^2\) with respect to the axis of rotation. The string pulls without slipping at a perpendicular distance of \(0.200\text{ m}\) from that axis. What is the tension in the string?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
the surface geometry/angle is not specified in text and appears to be needed from the figure
multi_step: solving for tension likely requires combining block motion with flywheel rotation
question_text includes friction coefficient, flywheel mass, moment of inertia, and string lever arm, indicating multiple interacting quantities
this is not a direct lookup; it suggests at least translational dynamics plus rotational dynamics linkage
multiple-choice options exist, but the requested answer is still a derived numeric value
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a concrete numeric target: '14.0 N'
choices provide an additional discrete check set including '14.0 N'
question_present=true and answer_present=true in asset registry

risk_flags: figure-dependent geometry is not recoverable from text alone
possible rounding/unit-format variation in numeric answer

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantics are available
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
high image dependency is adequately supported because the referenced figure exists
initial scores indicate strong multi-step reasoning value and good verifiability
